title: Inguinal canal
body_html:
The inguinal canal is a common source of questions and there are certain things candidates are expected to know:

  • Position of the canal
  • Composition of the roof, floor and walls
  • Position/planes of the deep (internal) and superficial inguinal rings
  • Contents

StructureContents of the inguinal canal
Nerves
  • 3 Nerves (Note ilioinguinal passes through superficial ring rather than truly through canal)
  • Genital branch of genitofemoral, ilioinguinal, autonomic fibres
Arteries
  • 3 Arteries
  • Artery to Vas Deferens, Cremasteric artery, testicular artery
Fascia
  • 3 Fascial Layers
  • external spermatic, cremasteric, internal spermatic
Other Things
  • 3 Other things
  • Pampiniform plexus, lymphatics, vas deferens (males)
Male/Female
  • Male: spermatic cord
  • Female: Round ligament of Uterus

Boundary of inguinal canalStructures composing boundary
Anterior wallAponeurosis of external oblique reinforced by internal oblique
Posterior wallTransversalis fascia
Conjoint tendon
Deep inguinal ring
FloorInguinal ligament
Lacunar ligament
RoofTransversalis fascia
Internal oblique
Transversus abdominis

Diagram illustrating inguinal canal


Diagrams illustrating inguinal canal and the position of the rings

Image sourced from Wikipedia

Deep inguinal ring
  • The entrance to the inguinal canal, the deep inguinal ring is located halfway between pubic tubercle and the anterior superior iliac spine
Superficial inguinal ring
  • The exit to the inguinal canal is located 1cm above and medial to the pubic tubercle

Diagram illustrating nerves of the inguinal canal


Diagrams illustrating direct vs indirect hernias and relationship to inguinal canal
